zh.Henlow, Bedfordshire, England: 英國英格蘭貝德福爾亨洛福
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment於1530年出生在英國貝德福爾亨洛福時，他的父親Henry Hurst25歲，母親Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant.26歲。他於1560年6月15日與Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer在英國賀特福爾巴內特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於從1570年6月3日到1571年6月30日在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.Henlow, Bedfordshire, England: 英国英格兰貝德福爾亨洛福
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment于1530年出生在英国貝德福爾亨洛福时，他的父亲Henry Hurst25岁，母亲Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant.26岁。他于1560年6月15日与Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer在英国賀特福爾巴內特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于从1570年6月3日到1571年6月30日在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Hurst
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): topographic name for someone who lived near a wood or wooded hill, from Middle English hirst(e), herst(e), hurst(e) (Old English hyrst) or a habitational name from any of the places so called, such as Hurst Green (in Mitton, Lancashire), Hirst (Northumberland), Hurst (Berkshire, Kent, Warwickshire), Hurstpierpoint (Sussex), or Hirst in Longwood (Yorkshire).
Irish: re-Anglicized form of de Horsaigh, the Gaelicized form of the English habitational name Horsey , established in Ireland since the 13th century.
German and Swiss German (also Hürst): topographic name from Middle High German hurst ‘woodland, thicket’; or a habitational name from a place so named in Westphalia.

variants.name: Herst
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or nickname from Polish herszt ‘ringleader, chieftain’.

variants.name: Horsey
meanings: English (southern): habitational name from Horsey Pignes (Somerset), Horse Eye (Sussex), or Horsey (Norfolk), from Old English hors ‘horse’ + ēg ‘island, low-lying land’.

variants.name: Hearst
meanings: Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of some similar (like-sounding) original Ashkenazic surname such as   Hirsch  .

variants.name: Horst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Phares, Alois, Armin, Fritz, Hans, Inge, Manfred, Monika, Reinhold.

North German and Dutch: topographic name from Middle Low German hurst, Middle Dutch horst ‘undergrowth, brushwood, wild place’. More specifically, the term was also used to denote a crow's or similar large bird's nest, a raised area in surrounding marshland, or an area of uncleared woodland, all of which meanings could have contributed to the surname. This surname is also a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word. Compare Hurst .

History: As a name of German origin (see 1 above) the surname Horst is established among the American Mennonites. Their progenitors were the brothers Joseph, Michael, and Peter Horst, who came to PA in 1731 together with their widowed mother. Among their descendants the altered form Hurst is also found.

variants.name: Hirst
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hurst  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment was born in 1530, in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, his father, Henry Hurst, was 25 and his mother, Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant., was 26. He married Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer on 15 June 1560, in Barnet, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died from 3 June 1570 to 30 June 1571, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment est né en 1530, à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry Hurst, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant., avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer le 15 juin 1560, à Barnet, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé du 3 juin 1570 au 30 juin 1571, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment 1530 in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry Hurst 25 und seine Mutter Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant. 26. Er hat am 15. Juni 1560 in Barnet, Hertfordshire, England Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb von 3. Juni 1570 bis 30. Juni 1571 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment è nato il nel 1530 nel Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, il suo padre, Henry Hurst, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant., aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer il 15 giugno 1560, nel Barnet, Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì da 3 giugno 1570 a 30 giugno 1571, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parlimentが1530年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウで生まれた時，父Henry Hurstは25歳，母Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant.は26歳であった。1560年6月15日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアバーネットでRose Marshe Agnes Fischerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼はから1570年6月3日まで1571年6月30日に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment(이)가 1530년 영국 베드퍼드실르 헨로에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Hurst(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant.(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1560년 6월 15일 영국 허트퍼드실르 바른에서 Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1570년 6월 3일부터 1571년 6월 30일까지 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment nasceu em 1530, em Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry Hurst, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant., tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer em 15 de junho de 1560, em Barnet, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu de 3 de junho de 1570 a 30 de junho de 1571, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment родился в 1530 г. в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry Hurst было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant., 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer 15 июня 1560 г. в местности барнет, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер с 3 июня 1570 по 30 июня 1571 в его родном городе.
